message.content:
Note: Amazon changes its HTML often and may block frequent/automated requests. Use reasonable delays and headers.

```python
import requests
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
import csv
import time

SEARCH_URL = "https://www.amazon.com/s"
PARAMS = {
    "k": "wireless mouse"  # change search term here
}
HEADERS = {
    "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) "
                  "AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) "
                  "Chrome/123.0.0.0 Safari/537.36",
    "Accept-Language": "en-US,en;q=0.9",
}

def fetch_page(url, params):
    time.sleep(2)
    r = requests.get(url, params=params, headers=HEADERS, timeout=15)
    r.raise_for_status()
    return r.text

def parse_products(html):
    soup = BeautifulSoup(html, "html.parser")
    results = []
    for item in soup.select("div.s-main-slot div[data-component-type='s-search-result']"):
        name_el = item.select_one("h2 a span")
        price_whole = item.select_one("span.a-price > span.a-offscreen")
        rating_el = item.select_one("span.a-icon-alt")

        name = name_el.get_text(strip=True) if name_el else ""
        price = price_whole.get_text(strip=True) if price_whole else ""
        rating = rating_el.get_text(strip=True) if rating_el else ""

        if name:
            results.append({
                "name": name,
                "price": price,
                "rating": rating,
            })
    return results

def save_to_csv(products, filename="amazon_products.csv"):
    fieldnames = ["name", "price", "rating"]
    with open(filename, "w", newline="", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        writer = csv.DictWriter(f, fieldnames=fieldnames)
        writer.writeheader()
        writer.writerows(products)

def main():
    html = fetch_page(SEARCH_URL, PARAMS)
    products = parse_products(html)
    save_to_csv(products)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```
